name: Yue Fei
description: Yue Fei (1103-1142) was a prominent military general who lived during the Southern Song dynasty of China. He is widely regarded as a patriot and national hero for his unwavering loyalty and exceptional military prowess in defending the Song dynasty from the invading Jin dynasty. Born into a peasant family, Yue Fei demonstrated extraordinary martial talent from a young age. He joined the army and quickly rose through the ranks, earning a reputation for his strategic brilliance and courage on the battlefield. Yue Fei's forces achieved numerous victories against the Jin, pushing them back and recovering lost territories. His most famous victory was the Battle of Yancheng, where he decisively defeated a much larger Jin army. Despite his military success and popular support, Yue Fei was framed for treason by the corrupt official Qin Hui and executed in 1142. His death sparked widespread outrage and cemented his status as a symbol of loyalty and patriotism. Yue Fei's story continues to inspire generations of Chinese people, and he is venerated as a model of integrity, courage, and unwavering devotion to his country. His legacy is preserved in numerous temples, stories, and artistic representations.
subtitle: Loyal General and Patriotic Hero
achievements: Defeated the Jin army at Yancheng
Recovered lost territories from the Jin
Became a symbol of loyalty and patriotism
Rose through the ranks due to his military prowess

keyMyths.title: Yue Fei's Tattoo
keyMyths.description: Yue Fei's mother tattooed the words 'Serve the country with utmost loyalty' on his back to remind him of his duty. This act symbolizes his unwavering patriotism and commitment to the Song dynasty. It's a powerful visual representation of his core values.

keyMyths.title: Battle of Yancheng
keyMyths.description: Yue Fei's victory at the Battle of Yancheng against a much larger Jin army is considered a pivotal moment in the war. His strategic brilliance and the bravery of his troops turned the tide and demonstrated the strength of the Song resistance. This battle cemented his reputation as a military genius.
keyMyths.source: Historical records of the Song dynasty

keyMyths.title: Framing and Execution
keyMyths.description: Yue Fei's unjust execution, orchestrated by the corrupt official Qin Hui, is a tragic event that highlights the dangers of political intrigue. Despite his innocence and contributions to the country, he was falsely accused of treason and put to death. This injustice fueled his legendary status.
keyMyths.source: Historical records and biographies of Yue Fei

associations.significance: Yue Fei's preferred weapon, symbolizing his martial prowess and leadership.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: A citizen-soldier who returns to private life after defending his country, embodying civic virtue.
A military leader driven by patriotism and divine inspiration, fighting for national liberation.

worshipPractices.name: Yue Fei Temple
worshipPractices.description: Temples dedicated to Yue Fei can be found throughout China. These temples serve as places of worship and remembrance, where people pay respects to Yue Fei's spirit and seek his blessings. Offerings, prayers, and ceremonies are conducted to honor his virtues and seek protection from harm.

worshipPractices.name: Folk Religion
worshipPractices.description: Yue Fei is venerated in Chinese folk religion as a deity who protects against injustice and misfortune. His image is often displayed in homes and businesses to ward off evil spirits and bring good fortune. People seek his guidance and intervention in times of trouble, viewing him as a symbol of righteousness and strength.

cultural.modernLegacy: Yue Fei's story continues to inspire generations of Chinese people. He is a symbol of patriotism, loyalty, and integrity. His story is retold in operas, literature, and films, reinforcing his values and virtues. Statues and monuments dedicated to him stand as reminders of his contributions to Chinese history. His spirit is invoked during times of national crisis.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Military Beginnings
extendedContent.content: Yue Fei was born in 1103 in Xiangzhou, Henan province, during a period of great turmoil in China. From a young age, he displayed exceptional physical strength and a keen interest in martial arts. Legend has it that his mother tattooed the words "Serve the country with utmost loyalty" on his back, a constant reminder of his duty. As the Jin dynasty encroached upon Song territory, Yue Fei felt compelled to join the army. He quickly rose through the ranks, demonstrating his military skills and strategic acumen. His early campaigns against local bandits and rebel forces honed his abilities and established his reputation as a capable commander. He emphasized discipline and training among his troops, fostering a strong sense of camaraderie and effectiveness. Yue Fei's early life laid the foundation for his later achievements as a national hero, driven by patriotism and a desire to protect his homeland.

extendedContent.title: Greatest Quests and Military Campaigns
extendedContent.content: Yue Fei's most significant contributions were his campaigns against the Jin dynasty, who had captured the Song capital and emperor. He led his army, known as the Yue Jia Jun, to numerous victories, recapturing lost territories and inflicting heavy losses on the enemy. His strategies were innovative, adapting to the terrain and utilizing the strengths of his soldiers. One notable campaign was the Battle of Yancheng, where he decisively defeated a much larger Jin army. He also implemented agricultural reforms in the territories he controlled, improving the lives of the local population and strengthening his support base. However, his relentless pursuit of victory and his unwavering determination to restore the Song dynasty led to conflicts with the court, particularly with the chancellor Qin Hui, who advocated for peace with the Jin.

extendedContent.title: Legacy and Enduring Symbolism
extendedContent.content: Despite his military successes, Yue Fei was tragically framed for treason and executed in 1142. This injustice transformed him into a symbol of patriotic resistance and a victim of political corruption. His story has been passed down through generations, inspiring countless individuals to stand up for their beliefs and defend their nation. Yue Fei's legacy extends beyond his military achievements. He is also remembered for his moral integrity, his dedication to his troops, and his unwavering loyalty to his country. His story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of political infighting and the importance of upholding justice. Temples and shrines dedicated to Yue Fei can be found throughout China, where he is revered as a national hero and a guardian against injustice. His spirit continues to resonate with people today, reminding them of the values of patriotism, loyalty, and righteousness.

shortDescription: Yue Fei (1103-1142) was a Song Dynasty general and national hero, famed for his loyalty and military genius in defending against the Jin, though ultimately betrayed and executed.
